stack.rendering: Jinja server-rendered HTML with Bootstrap and small browser-side JavaScript
stack.desktop_shell: PyWebView starting point for an agent-led desktop conversion
stack.production_server: Gunicorn building block for an agent-led private Linux deployment

data_topology.registry: One meta.sqlite registry stores users and ledger paths
data_topology.ledgers: Each selected user has a separate SQLite ledger

financial_invariants: Money is stored and calculated as integer cents
Expenses and transfer-out legs are negative; income and transfer-in legs are positive
Transfers are atomically linked equal-and-opposite pairs
Transaction splits sum exactly to the signed parent amount except explicit service-owned unallocated cases
Account-locked envelopes are used only with their compatible account
Reconciled transaction mutation protections are preserved
Planning previews are side-effect free and writes require explicit confirmation
Multi-table writes use a service and one unit_of_work transaction

branding.stable_identity_rule: Do not rename data-directory slugs, storage keys, event names, service IDs, or persistent identifiers as part of visible branding without a migration plan

customization_surfaces.terminology: Visible Jinja copy and user-facing documentation; persisted terms and stable identifiers require separate risk review
customization_surfaces.workflows: Blueprint plus service plus repositories or migration when persistent, with focused tests

verification_commands.purpose: Run the standard coding-agent configuration, privacy, source, and theme preflight
Validate structured customization profiles and machine-readable project references
Require a completed local customization brief to be implementation-ready
verification_commands.condition: Run only after agent-config/customization-profile.local.json has been created

verification_commands.purpose: Validate the tracked theme profile and generated stylesheet
Compile application and agent helper Python
Create a new fictional demonstration workspace
verification_commands.writes: Creates only the named new destination and refuses an existing destination

verification_commands.purpose: Verify the managed workspace, SQLite invariants, and users/dashboard/savings smoke path
verification_commands.writes: Direct SQLite inspection is read-only; default Flask smoke may apply idempotent schema or metadata repair. Pass --no-smoke for read-only inspection.

verification_commands.purpose: Validate local launch configuration without starting a blocking server
Run the regression suite with generated isolated synthetic databases
verification_commands.writes: Temporary synthetic test directories plus ignored __pycache__ bytecode unless Python bytecode writing is disabled

passed: Direct-command schema workspace bootstrap and doctor
Direct-command fictional-demo workspace bootstrap and doctor
Users page and selected-user dashboard HTTP 200 smoke checks
Disposable local server/browser workflow at desktop and 390-pixel mobile widths
Savings preview, one grouped transfer recording, and paired transaction verification
Full synthetic regression suite: 637 tests passed
Downloaded-repository full preflight: repository safety, schema/demo bootstrap, doctors, launcher, and regression suite passed
Reviewed repository snapshot: current-tree and reachable-history safety checks passed
Five workspace bootstrap, refusal, boundary, and doctor tests
Existing-destination refusal with database SHA-256 preserved
Managed-workspace backup/restore round trip, online SQLite snapshots, upload preservation, overwrite/path-escape/tamper refusal, and restored demo doctor passed (5 focused tests)
verification_evidence.scope_limit: Disposable local Windows browser workflow and synthetic backup/restore round trip only; not a clean-machine install, owner-data recovery exercise, EXE, or server deployment verification
